data.title: Altcoin crash puts 100+ ETF applications on ice as Wall Street scrambles
data.description:
Wall Street’s attempt to cash in on the altcoin wave just hit a wall. Over 130 ETF applications tied to risky crypto tokens are now effectively frozen, after a violent crash last week erased billions of dollars and exposed how thin, unstable, and illiquid most of these coins still are. The same applications that flooded […]

data.title: Today, the US Bitcoin ETF had a net outflow of 4,998 BTC, while the Ethereum ETF had a net inflow of 4,202 ETH.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, today 10 US Bitcoin ETFs had a net outflow of 4,998 BTC, of ​​which ARK 21Shares had an outflow of 2,544 BTC, and currently holds 42,474 BTC; 9 Ethereum ETFs had a net inflow of 4,202 ETH, of which BlackRock had an inflow of 12,098 ETH, and currently holds 4,052,934 ETH.

data.title: Analysis: The road to the upside for risk assets will be more turbulent
data.description: Trading information platform Kobeissi Letter published an article on the X platform stating that US investor leverage has nearly doubled over the past two years, a similar rate of growth to the post-pandemic period in 2020. In September, US investors took on an additional $67 billion in margin debt, bringing the total to a record $1.13 trillion. 5x leveraged ETFs for US stocks have just been submitted to the SEC. These ETFs will cover everything from Nvidia to cryptocurrencies. This means that if Nvidia drops 10% in a single day, the leveraged ETFs will drop 50%, making the upward trajectory of risky assets even more volatile. Risk appetite is at an all-time high, with everyone eager to participate in the AI ​​revolution. US household stock allocations have reached a record 52%, exceeding the 2000 peak of 48% by 4 percentage points and doubling the 2008 low. Americans are flocking to the stock market.

data.title: Ethena Labs Launches Stablecoin Service on Conduit Platform
data.description:
According to Foresight News, Ethena Labs has announced the launch of its stablecoin-as-a-service stack, Ethena Whitelabel, on the cross-border payment service provider Conduit. This integration allows chains built using Conduit to access Ethena's infrastructure and create their own stablecoins during deployment.

Conduit supports 55% of chains on Ethereum, with a total locked value exceeding $4 billion across more than 60 mainnet deployments. It powers decentralized finance (DeFi), real-world assets (RWAs), and consumer applications.

data.title: Charles Schwab Reports Surge in Interest for Cryptocurrency Products
data.description: According to PANews, Charles Schwab, a financial services company managing approximately $11 trillion in assets, has observed a significant increase in user interest in its cryptocurrency-related products. The company's cryptocurrency website has experienced a 90% year-over-year increase in visits, reflecting growing engagement in the digital asset sector.

data.title: Analysts: Bitcoin's extreme correction range may be between $98,000 and $104,000, and bottom-fishing traders are preparing to "hit the ball"
data.description: On-chain data analyst Murphy shared the market's chip distribution using the UTXO Realized Price Distribution (URPD): Compared to yesterday's data, URPD saw an increase of 53,000 BTC around $104,700, approaching the "extreme retracement range of $98,000 to $104,000." Traders looking to buy the dip are preparing to pounce. Yesterday's data showed a period of selling at the high end of the $117,000 range, with a new, massive column forming at $112,000. This indicates a significant amount of capital is bottom-fishing here, with a total accumulation of 614,000 BTC, currently the highest column in the overall chip structure. If BTC continues to fluctuate downward, based on the current chip structure, Murphy predicts that "the extreme retracement range will be between $98,000 and $104,000. Of course, the actual situation will be influenced by macroeconomic policies, market sentiment, and unforeseen black swan events."

data.title: Chainalysis: European crypto adoption enters a "network effect acceleration period," with MiCA and local stablecoins becoming key drivers
data.description: According to Chainalysis' "2025 Crypto Geography Report," Europe's overall crypto transaction volume, after a pullback in mid-2024, peaked at $234 billion in December of the same year and continued to grow into the first half of 2025, demonstrating a mature market landscape characterized by both institutional activity and retail adoption. The report shows that Russia leapt to the top of the European trading volume with $376.3 billion, followed by the UK ($273.2 billion) and Germany ($219.4 billion). Germany saw a 54% year-over-year increase, benefiting from the implementation of MiCA regulations and the entry of financial institutions. Poland and Ukraine saw increases of 51% and 52%, respectively, highlighting the activeness of private remittances and grassroots adoption. In the ten months since MiCA's implementation, Europe has transitioned from fragmented regulation to a unified framework. Circle's Euro stablecoin, EURC, saw a 2727% increase during this period, replacing USDT as the dominant regulated stablecoin. The European Securities and Markets Authority (ESMA) currently has 15 registered e-money token issuers, managing a total of 25 single-currency stablecoins. The report notes that the European market exhibits a paradoxical trend of "larger size, faster growth," indicating that the crypto ecosystem is in the accelerating S-curve phase, with network effects driving continued expansion in mature markets. The UK market, on the other hand, is shifting towards DeFi platforms, with retail funds flowing into DEXs, while institutions still prefer centralized trading venues. Overall, the European crypto ecosystem is entering a new phase of balancing regulation and innovation: MiCA has fostered the localization of stablecoins, DeFi usage has surged, and institutional participation has deepened, steadily elevating Europe's position in the global crypto ecosystem.

data.title: OneKey responds to the Milk Sad incident: The vulnerability does not affect the security of its software and hardware wallets
data.description: According to OneKey's Chinese Twitter account, regarding the random number vulnerability implicated in the recent "Milk Sad" incident, the OneKey team clarified that the vulnerability does not affect the security of mnemonics and private keys in OneKey's software and hardware wallets. The vulnerability stems from Libbitcoin Explorer (bx) version 3.x, which uses a pseudo-random number generator based on the system time and the Mersenne Twister-32 algorithm. This seed space is limited to 2³² bits, allowing attackers to derive private keys through prediction or brute force. This vulnerability affects some older versions of Trust Wallet and all products using bx 3.x or older versions of Trust Wallet Core. OneKey stated that its hardware wallets utilize an EAL6+ security chip with a built-in TRNG true random number generator; older devices have also passed SP800-22 and FIPS140-2 entropy testing. The software wallet uses a system-level CSPRNG entropy source to generate random numbers, complying with cryptographic standards. The team emphasized that users are advised to use hardware wallets to manage their assets and should not import mnemonics generated by software wallets into hardware wallets to ensure maximum security.

data.title: Trump calls his 100% China tariff “not sustainable,” pushes for fair deal
data.description:
U. S. President Donald Trump admitted on Friday that his 100% tariff on Chinese goods cannot hold forever, but insisted Beijing left him no choice. Speaking on Fox Business Network, Trump said, “It’s not sustainable, but that’s what the number is. They forced me to do that. ” The comment came as Washington and Beijing remain stuck […]
source: https://www.cryptopolitan.com/trump-calls-100-china-tariff-unsustainable/
